title: 2008 Financial Crisis
content: The 2008 financial crisis was triggered by the collapse of the US housing bubble. Lehman Brothers filed for bankruptcy on September 15, 2008, the largest bankruptcy in US history at the time. The crisis led to the Great Recession (2007-2009). The US government passed the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act creating TARP, a $700 billion bailout program.

title: GDP Rankings
content: As of 2024, the largest economies by nominal GDP are: United States (~$28 trillion), China (~$18 trillion), Germany (~$4.6 trillion), Japan (~$4.2 trillion), and India (~$3.9 trillion). The European Union collectively would be second largest. GDP figures vary by source and measurement method (nominal vs PPP).

title: Bitcoin
content: Bitcoin was created by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto, whose whitepaper was published on October 31, 2008. The first Bitcoin block (genesis block) was mined on January 3, 2009. The first known commercial transaction was on May 22, 2010 when Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas (now celebrated as Bitcoin Pizza Day).

title: Federal Reserve
content: The Federal Reserve System was created on December 23, 1913, when President Woodrow Wilson signed the Federal Reserve Act. It is the central banking system of the United States. The Fed has a dual mandate: maximum employment and stable prices. The federal funds rate was near zero (0-0.25%) from March 2020 to March 2022.

title: Nobel Prize in Economics
content: The Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences was established in 1968 by Sweden's central bank (Sveriges Riksbank) and first awarded in 1969 to Ragnar Frisch and Jan Tinbergen. It is technically not one of the original Nobel Prizes established by Alfred Nobel's will in 1895. Notable winners include Paul Samuelson (1970), Milton Friedman (1976), and Daniel Kahneman (2002).

title: Inflation History
content: Hyperinflation in the Weimar Republic (Germany) peaked in November 1923, with prices doubling every 3.7 days. Zimbabwe experienced hyperinflation peaking at approximately 79.6 billion percent month-on-month in November 2008. Venezuela experienced annual inflation exceeding 1,000,000% in 2018. Hungary holds the record for highest monthly inflation: 41.9 quadrillion percent in July 1946.

title: World Trade Organization
content: The World Trade Organization (WTO) was established on January 1, 1995, replacing the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT). It is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland. As of 2024, the WTO has 164 member countries. China joined the WTO on December 11, 2001. Russia joined on August 22, 2012.

title: Stock Market History
content: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) was founded on May 17, 1792 with the Buttonwood Agreement. Black Monday occurred on October 19, 1987 when the Dow Jones fell 22.6% in one day. The dot-com bubble peaked in March 2000 and the NASDAQ lost 78% of its value by October 2002. The Dow Jones first closed above 10,000 on March 29, 1999.

title: European Central Bank
content: The European Central Bank (ECB) was established on June 1, 1998 and is headquartered in Frankfurt, Germany. The euro was introduced as an accounting currency on January 1, 1999 and euro banknotes and coins entered circulation on January 1, 2002. The ECB's primary objective is to maintain price stability in the eurozone.

title: OPEC
content: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) was founded on September 14, 1960 by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela. As of 2024, OPEC has 12 member countries. OPEC+ (including Russia and other non-OPEC producers) was formed in 2016. The 1973 oil embargo by Arab OPEC members quadrupled oil prices.
